response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with 18+ years of development experience and genuine developer-tooling credentials (test runners, assertion libraries, CI/CD infrastructure, MCP code generators), which aligns well with the engineering-productivity focus. However, this is a Level II people-management role requiring 2+ years of formal people management and 3+ years of technical leadership, and the resume shows no evidence of managing engineers, running one-on-ones, owning performance cycles, or leading organizational change. Teaching workshops and founding a solo open-source project do not substitute for people leadership at this level.
strengths: Deep developer-tools experience: built test runners (Trial), assertion libraries (Fluent-Asserts), CI/CD and dev-ops infrastructure
18+ years of broad software development across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems
Demonstrated mentorship inclination via workshops, Selenium courses, and code kata facilitation
concerns: No formal people-management experience — a hard minimum requirement for this role
No demonstrated experience leading organizational change or setting team-level roadmaps
Mobile experience (iOS/Apple TV) is dated (2015-2016) and shallow relative to a mobile-platform ecosystem focus

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling and testing infrastructure background
